questionHtml:
Nitrogen monoxide reacts with oxygen gas to form nitrogen dioxide.

Deduce, giving a reason, whether the following mechanism is possible.
markschemeHtml:
not possible AND «proposed» mechanism does not match experimental rate law
OR
not possible AND «proposed» mechanism shows zero/not first order with respect to oxygen ✔

meta.title: Nitrogen monoxide reacts with oxygen gas to form nitrogen dioxide. Deduce, giving a reason, whether the following mechanism is possible.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 2. How much, how fast and how far?
Reactivity 2.2—How fast? The rate of chemical change

Reactivity 2.2.6—Many reactions occur in a series of elementary steps. The slowest step determines the rate of the reaction. Evaluate proposed reaction mechanisms and recognize reaction intermediates. Distinguish between intermediates and transition states, and recognize both in energy profiles of reactions.
